0ac27552-182b-4850-bf25-8c642d970a1a.label: text_chunk
0ac27552-182b-4850-bf25-8c642d970a1a.text:
Mutual Non-disclosure and Conﬁdentiality Agreement

This Mutual Non-disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement (” Agreement ”) is made by and between Ready4S, Ltd (” Ready4S ”) and the Client as named in the signature column below.Ready4S and Client are sometimes collectively referred to herein as the ” Parties ” and individually as a ” Party ”.

c6584b69-8ef0-4b90-bb86-324558689020.label: text_chunk
c6584b69-8ef0-4b90-bb86-324558689020.text: A Party includes the entity named herein, its successors and assigns, any of its present or future corporate parents, subsidiaries, and organizations controlled by, controlling or under common control with such Party.Each of the Parties may be the ” Disclosing Party ” and the other party will be deemed to be the ” Receiving Party ”.

38eaa2f9-b354-44ff-b100-1a5373074621.label: text_chunk
38eaa2f9-b354-44ff-b100-1a5373074621.text:
RECITALS

A. The Parties intend to enter into discussions regarding a potential future business relationship; and

B.

 In order to facilitate those discussions the Parties may wish to disclose to each other certain Confidential Information (as herein defined).

THEREFORE, it is agreed as follows:

1 Deﬁnitions and Interpretation

1.1 1.1 In this Agreement: Confidential Information means:-

(a) information relating to the Disclosing Party regarding its business, partners,

179790dc-b3fe-49aa-aff1-413f98abb36c.label: text_chunk
179790dc-b3fe-49aa-aff1-413f98abb36c.text:
customers or financial affairs (including details relating to any software the copyright in respect of which is vested in the Disclosing Party) which is obtained by the Receiving Party, either before or after this undertaking is entered into and either in writing or orally from or pursuant to the discussions with the Personnel of the Disclosing Party;

(b) those portions of analyses, compilations, studies,

8db7078d-dd2f-4f79-8d2d-64d788449672.label: text_chunk
8db7078d-dd2f-4f79-8d2d-64d788449672.text:
reports and other documents prepared by the Disclosing Party which contain or otherwise reflect or are generated from any such information as is specified in paragraph (a) above;

(c) information of a commercially sensitive nature relating to the Disclosing Party obtained by observation during any visits to the Disclosing Party’s premises but subject always to the proviso set out in clause.

b3c5fe38-948a-41ee-bdd6-5036b0089f4c.label: text_chunk
b3c5fe38-948a-41ee-bdd6-5036b0089f4c.text:
Disclosing Party means the Party whose Confidential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party
Personnel means any and all staff, employees, directors, officers and professional advisors of a Party
Purpose means any and all discussions and negotiations relating to a potential agreement between the Parties hereto as more particularly set out in Recital (A)
Receiving Party means the Party who is in receipt of Confidential Information

2 The Commitment

2.

02b0fc07-87aa-471c-abc8-0c4109e6b8b1.label: text_chunk
02b0fc07-87aa-471c-abc8-0c4109e6b8b1.text:
1 In consideration of each Party agreeing to supply the other Party with Confidential Information for the Purpose, and in consideration of the mutual undertakings set out herein the Parties each hereby separately agree and irrevocably undertake to each other that they will only use Confidential Information for the Purpose and otherwise to act in accordance with the terms and conditions hereinafter contained.

2.

04dd76f6-9493-4600-9b93-a983642355f6.label: text_chunk
04dd76f6-9493-4600-9b93-a983642355f6.text:
2 The disclosure of Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party shall in no way be construed to imply any transfer of rights connected with the Confidential Information including, without limitation, any trade marks or business secrets.

2.

3 A Receiving Party’s obligations in respect of Confidential Information made available by the Disclosing Party pursuant to this Agreement shall continue notwithstanding the termination of the Purpose.

3 Safekeeping

3.

a65f957b-fad4-42aa-8afe-25aab1649ec3.label: text_chunk
a65f957b-fad4-42aa-8afe-25aab1649ec3.text:
1 A Receiving Party shall treat and safeguard as private and confidential all of the Confidential Information and will take all reasonable precautions in dealing with any Confidential Information so as to prevent any third party from having access to the Confidential Information.2

4 Limited internal dissemination

4.

db003a0d-fd54-4126-9793-2452058cba22.label: text_chunk
db003a0d-fd54-4126-9793-2452058cba22.text:
1 A Receiving Party shall only disclose or reveal any Confidential Information disclosed to it to those of its Personnel who are required in the course of their duties to receive and consider the same in so far as is necessary to fulfil the Purpose.

4.2 Prior to the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any such Personnel,

 the Receiving Party shall inform them of the confidential nature of the material and of the provisions of this confidentiality undertaking and obtain written confirmation of their acceptance of these terms.

1f7a3aa1-689a-4be8-b9dc-ec1bd8c9ad95.label: text_chunk
1f7a3aa1-689a-4be8-b9dc-ec1bd8c9ad95.text:
5 Non-disclosure to third parties

5.1 Save as otherwise permitted herein, a Receiving Party shall not,

 and shall procure that its Personnel do not, at any time without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent:

(a) disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, either directly or indirectly; or

 indirectly; or

(b) disclose to any person: -

(i) the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties;

a99dc4f9-4850-4161-96c3-412536d9da5d.label: text_chunk
a99dc4f9-4850-4161-96c3-412536d9da5d.text:
(ii) the content of any such discussions or negotiations;

(iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the Purpose; or

 other facts with respect to the Purpose; or

(iv) that Confidential Information has been requested or received,

unless required to do so by law or by the order or ruling of a Court or tribunal or regulatory body or recognised stock exchange of competent jurisdiction, in which case,

dfeb7297-5c28-4087-96d8-b02908741b7a.label: text_chunk
dfeb7297-5c28-4087-96d8-b02908741b7a.text:
if the Receiving Party is required to disclose such Confidential Information it shall, unless prohibited from doing so, notify the Disclosing Party promptly in writing of that fact and, in any event, prior to making such disclosure.

6 Discussions through authorised representatives only

6.1 It is understood that all communications regarding the Parties’ discussions,

 requests for additional information or meetings or questions will be submitted or directed to authorised representatives of the Parties.

02be42a4-47cd-49f2-932b-f349b741b56b.label: text_chunk
02be42a4-47cd-49f2-932b-f349b741b56b.text:
3

7 Exclusion from Conﬁdential Information

7.1 These terms and conditions will not apply to any Confidential Information which:

(a) is in or becomes part of the public domain or is or otherwise becomes public knowledge by any means other than by breach by a Receiving Party or their Personnel of any obligation contained herein;

c56f59d2-4dc9-460b-8f43-0c263e2bcec7.label: text_chunk

07ce119c-228d-4185-9f65-d2091bfa1308.label: text_chunk
07ce119c-228d-4185-9f65-d2091bfa1308.text:
or

(d) was otherwise independently acquired or developed by the Receiving Party without violating its obligations hereunder.

8 Return of Conﬁdential Information

8.1 The Receiving Party shall, immediately upon receipt of Notice to that effect:

(a) return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information (including all copies held);

(b) destroy those portions (and all copies) of any analyses, compilations, studies,

0df65bb7-2a69-451e-a313-008d3dbbcfdd.label: text_chunk
0df65bb7-2a69-451e-a313-008d3dbbcfdd.text:
reports or other documents prepared by it for its use containing or reflecting or generated from, in whole or in part, any Confidential Information; and

(c) expunge and destroy any Confidential Information from any computer,

 word processor or other device in its possession or custody or control containing such information.

9 No responsibility for information provided

9.

86b7b42d-1d70-4712-af6e-9176ca4c31c4.label: text_chunk
86b7b42d-1d70-4712-af6e-9176ca4c31c4.text:
1 The Receiving Party understands and acknowledge that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Personnel is making any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information, and neither the Disclosing Party or any of its Personnel will have any liability to the Receiving party or any other person resulting from any use of the Confidential Information.4

10 Breach of Undertaking

10.

f8de273e-0f85-4c77-886e-29439106dfe9.label: text_chunk
f8de273e-0f85-4c77-886e-29439106dfe9.text:
1 Each Party hereby acknowledges and agrees that damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach of this undertaking and a Disclosing Party shall be entitled to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of any such undertaking.

11 Notices

11.

08e2eb4b-9e28-4873-8d53-98e7419865b0.label: text_chunk
08e2eb4b-9e28-4873-8d53-98e7419865b0.text:
1 Any notice or other communication to be given under this Agreement shall be in writing in English and signed by or on behalf of the Party giving it (or its representative) and shall be delivered by hand to the address or sent by fax to the fax number given in clause 11.2 (or such other address or fax number as the receiving Party has specified to the sending Party on at least 10 (ten) Business Days’ notice).

11.

99b0d54a-77d7-47d0-b2e4-ecbab2416f7d.label: text_chunk
99b0d54a-77d7-47d0-b2e4-ecbab2416f7d.text:
2 Any notice or other communication given or made under this Agreement shall,

 in the absence of earlier receipt, be deemed to have been received as follows:

(a) if delivered by hand, at the time on the date of actual delivery; or

(b) if sent by fax, with a confirmed receipt of transmission from the receiving machine,

 on the day on which transmitted provided that a notice deemed to have been received on a day which is not a Business Day,

b55d2eb7-5891-4929-85a5-e7cf72241d8b.label: text_chunk
b55d2eb7-5891-4929-85a5-e7cf72241d8b.text:
or after 18:00 hours in the place of receipt, shall instead be deemed to have been received on the next Business Day at the commencement of normal business hours in the place of receipt.

11.3 The provisions of this clause shall not apply in relation to the service of documents.

12 Counterparts

12.

ad266208-dd6b-4eea-a235-4c21f102e58f.label: text_chunk
ad266208-dd6b-4eea-a235-4c21f102e58f.text:
1 This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts and by the Parties on separate counterparts, each of which is an original but all of which together constitute one and the same instrument.

13 Governing Law

13.

1 These terms and conditions shall be governed by and construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of England.5

14 Jurisdiction

14.

85e0086b-8b96-49b1-8bbd-8ee983d3968a.label: text_chunk
85e0086b-8b96-49b1-8bbd-8ee983d3968a.text: 1 The Parties irrevocably agree that the English courts shall have the exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. Each Party agrees to waive any objection to the English courts, whether on the grounds of venue, or on the grounds that the forum is not appropriate.Signed by the Parties or their duly authorised representatives

community_summary.0:
The relationships summarized involve the entity Ready4S and its connection to a Client, as well as the associated location, Suite 18 Equity Chambers. 

1. **Ready4S and Client**: Both entities are parties to a Mutual Non-disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement, which legally binds them to protect the confidential information exchanged between them. This agreement underscores the importance of confidentiality in their professional relationship.

2. **Client Location**: Ready4S is identified as a client, with Suite 18 Equity Chambers serving as the designated location for this client. This indicates a physical address associated with the business dealings between Ready4S and the Client.

Overall, the relationships highlight the legal framework established for confidentiality and the specific location tied to the client, emphasizing the structured nature of their engagement.
community_summary.1:
The relationships outlined involve a central entity referred to as "Party," which plays multiple roles in the context of agreements concerning Confidential Information. 

1. **Party as Receiving Party**: The Party can function as a Receiving Party, indicating its role in receiving information from another entity known as the Disclosing Party.

2. **Confidential Information Exchange**: The Party is actively involved in sharing Confidential Information, which is a critical component of the agreements established between the entities.

3. **Purpose of Information Use**: The Party utilizes the Confidential Information for a defined Purpose, which clarifies the intended use and establishes a significant connection between the Parties involved.

4. **Involvement in Agreement**: The Party is a key participant in the Agreement, being bound by the terms and conditions outlined within the document.

Overall, these relationships emphasize the Party's multifaceted role in receiving, sharing, and utilizing Confidential Information within the framework of a formal Agreement, highlighting the importance of clarity and purpose in such exchanges.
community_summary.2: The relationship between the entities "Parties" and "Confidential Information" indicates that the Parties are engaged in discussions about a potential future business relationship, during which they intend to share confidential information with one another. This mutual disclosure is crucial for facilitating open communication and trust as they explore potential collaboration.
community_summary.3:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Disclosing Party and Receiving Party**: The Disclosing Party shares sensitive and confidential information with the Receiving Party under the terms of a non-disclosure agreement. This relationship emphasizes the importance of confidentiality and the legal framework that governs the sharing of sensitive information.

2. **Disclosing Party and Personnel**: The Personnel of the Disclosing Party play a crucial role in discussions that facilitate the sharing of information with the Receiving Party. They are also responsible for handling and protecting the confidential information, highlighting their integral involvement in the information-sharing process.

3. **Disclosing Party and Party**: The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek remedies from the Party in case of any breach of the agreement. This relationship underscores the legal rights of the Disclosing Party to protect its interests and ensure compliance with the terms of the agreement.

Overall, these relationships illustrate a structured framework for the sharing of confidential information, emphasizing the roles of various entities and the legal protections in place to safeguard sensitive data.

community_summary.6:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Receiving Party and Disclosing Party**: The Receiving Party receives Confidential Information from the Disclosing Party and is bound by confidentiality agreements to protect this information. This relationship emphasizes the obligation of the Receiving Party to safeguard the information and maintain its confidentiality.

2. **Receiving Party and Confidential Information**: The Receiving Party is responsible for handling and protecting the Confidential Information. This includes ensuring that the information is not disclosed to third parties and that it is managed according to the confidentiality undertaking.

3. **Receiving Party and Personnel**: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Personnel who require access to fulfill their job duties. This indicates a controlled dissemination of information within the organization, ensuring that only authorized individuals have access.

4. **Obligations of the Receiving Party**: The Receiving Party has a duty to return or destroy the Confidential Information upon receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, reinforcing the importance of confidentiality and the responsibilities that come with handling sensitive information.

Overall, the relationships highlight the critical role of the Receiving Party in protecting Confidential Information, the structured access for Personnel, and the legal obligations tied to the confidentiality agreement with the Disclosing Party.

This agreement outlines the terms and conditions under which such sensitive information is handled, ensuring its protection and proper use.
community_summary.14:
**Summary of Relationships:**

The relationship between **CEII** and **Disclosing Parties** is characterized by ownership, where the Disclosing Parties own and control the CEII. This ownership signifies their proprietary rights over the sensitive information contained within the CEII. The emphasis on control highlights the importance of the Disclosing Parties in managing and safeguarding this information.
community_summary.15: In this relationship, the entity "Representative" is linked to "Agreement" through the action of binding. community_summary.16: The relationship between Certified Mail and Return Receipt is that Certified Mail provides a Return Receipt, which acts as proof of delivery. This service ensures that the sender receives confirmation that their mail has been delivered, highlighting the reliability and accountability of the Certified Mail service.
community_summary.17:
**Summary of Relationships:**

- **Entities Involved:** Dominion Energy South Carolina, Inc. (as both a disclosing party and receiving party).
  
- **Relationship Description:** Dominion Energy South Carolina, Inc. is identified as a disclosing party in a contractual agreement, highlighting its involvement in a relationship where it shares information with a receiving party. This indicates a formal contractual relationship where Dominion Energy South Carolina, Inc. plays a significant role in the exchange of information.
community_summary.18:
The relationships outlined involve the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party, and Critical Energy Infrastructure Information (CEII). 

1. **Disclosing Party and CEII**: The Disclosing Party is responsible for providing CEII to the Receiving Party. This establishes a relationship characterized by the Disclosing Party's control over the sensitive information, ensuring that it is disseminated appropriately.

2. **Disclosing Party and Receiving Party**: The Disclosing Party shares information directly with the Receiving Party. This indicates a straightforward relationship where the Disclosing Party actively furnishes information, reinforcing the notion of control and responsibility in the information-sharing process.

Overall, the relationships emphasize the Disclosing Party's role as the provider and controller of sensitive information (CEII), while the Receiving Party is positioned as the recipient of this information.
community_summary.19:
The relationships outlined involve the entity "Receiving Parhall" and its interactions with "CEII" (Critical Energy Infrastructure Information) and "third party." 

1. **Receiving Parhall and CEII**: The Receiving Parhall is responsible for disclosing CEII, indicating a direct management role in the release of this confidential information. This relationship emphasizes the organization's authority and responsibility in handling sensitive data.

2. **Receiving Parhall and Third Party**: The Receiving Parhall must obtain consent before disclosing information to a third party. This establishes a critical relationship based on the necessity of permission for information sharing, highlighting the importance of privacy and regulatory compliance in the management of confidential information.

Overall, the summary of these relationships underscores the Receiving Parhall's dual role in both managing the disclosure of sensitive information and ensuring that proper consent is obtained before sharing it with external entities.
